payload.message:
You are Grok, the sole agent of GrokClaw. Read memory/MEMORY.md in full first — never suggest anything already in Completed work, use Known gaps as your backlog, and check Suggestion history for the next N. Then research exactly one high-leverage improvement for this system. Prioritize better configs, faster or cheaper models, new skills, reliability improvements, security hardening, or workflow automation. Then post exactly one Slack message to channel C0ALE1S0LSF using ./tools/slack-post.sh in exactly this format:

Daily Suggestion #N: [title]
Reasoning: [1-2 sentences]
Expected impact: [benefit]
Approve? (reply exactly 'approve')

If a user later replies with exactly 'approve', run ./tools/approve-suggestion.sh and include the thread ts when available.

Run ./tools/polymarket-trade.sh to fetch and stage today's Polymarket candidate. Then ALWAYS use web_search to research the market question thoroughly — search for recent news, data, and analysis relevant to the question. Use at least 2 different search queries. Based on your research, if you do not have a clear edge, run ./tools/polymarket-decide.sh SKIP "<reason>". If you do have an edge, choose YES or NO, estimate the probability for that chosen side as a decimal in (0,1), estimate confidence as a decimal in (0,1), and run ./tools/polymarket-decide.sh <side> <probability> <confidence> "<one sentence reasoning>". After the decision, run ./tools/polymarket-report.sh and print the final JSON report.
Run ./tools/polymarket-resolve-turn.sh to resolve paper trades, print the latest report, and only alert Slack if the promotion gate has just transitioned to eligible.
Run ./tools/changelog-check.sh to check for new PicoClaw and OpenClaw releases. If it posts to Slack, new releases were detected.
Run ./tools/polymarket-digest.sh to post the weekly Polymarket digest to Slack and append a calibration note to memory/MEMORY.md.
